name: Warden of Beasts
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Flesh, worth 5,500 GC)
description: You trace the sigil of the Primal Wyrm — eldest of the flesh-blooded dragons, whose shed scales became the first beasts — and roar a command in the Old Tongue. Up to 3 willing or subdued creatures you can see within range transform into beasts of your choosing (CR 4 or lower). The draconic essence in their blood awakens their feral ancestry, reshaping bone and sinew in a flash of amber light. Transformed creatures retain their alignment and personality, and their game statistics become those of the chosen beast — yet their eyes retain the slitted pupils of the dragon-blooded. An unwilling creature of 75%+ purity may make a Wisdom saving throw to resist. If any transformed creature drops to 0 hit points, it reverts with its original hit points less any excess damage, as the draconic thread severs.
higher_level: When cast at 9th level, up to 5 creatures may be targeted and the CR limit increases to 5.

name: Null-Field
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 12,000 GC)
description: You carve your bloodline sigil into the air with a blade of condensed void-essence, and the world's magic gutters in a sphere around you. A shimmering 10-foot-radius sphere of aetheric dead-zone surrounds and moves with you, remaining centered on you for the duration. Within the sphere, spells cannot be cast, summoned creatures wink out of existence, and magic items function only as mundane objects. Aether-Cores within the zone pulse with a sickly gray light but produce no power. Creatures of 90%+ purity who enter the zone feel physically ill — their bloodline screams against the void. Spells and magical effects of 9th level or higher are unaffected by the null-field. If a creature's summoned form or ongoing magical effect would be suppressed, it flickers back into existence the instant it leaves the sphere. Inquisitors carry portable null-field generators powered by chained Aether-Cores — this spell was reverse-engineered from their technology by a Sovereign who died the day after perfecting it.

name: Blood-Binding Aversion
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Flesh, worth 7,000 GC, saturated with drops of the caster's own blood)
description: You press your bleeding palm against the target object or 200-foot-cube space and whisper the name of your progenitor dragon, binding its essence into the fabric of the place. Choose a creature type (or a specific bloodline sigil). Creatures of that type who enter the area must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be overcome with either irresistible attraction or profound repulsion (your choice at casting). Those attracted spend each turn moving closer, unable to willingly move away; those repulsed are frightened and use their movement to flee. A creature who succeeds on the save is immune to the effect for 24 hours, though they still feel the pull — a faint itch in their blood that will not fade. The effect permeates walls, floors, and doors. An Inquisitor who enters a space warded against their bloodline cannot hide their discomfort; their movements become stiff, their breathing audible. This spell was once used by a dying dragon to seal her lair for a century.

name: Blood-Forged Simulacrum
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Flesh, worth 15,000 GC, and a vessel of blood drawn from the target — at least 1 pint per 10 hit points)
description: You bleed the target — or yourself — and shape a vessel from crystallized blood, bone, and the raw aether of the Flesh-Father dragon lineage. Over the hour, this grotesque chrysalis hardens, cracks, and births a perfect duplicate: a living, breathing creature identical to the original in every respect save one — it is sterile, soulless, and knows it. The duplicate has the same statistics, memories, and personality as the original at the moment of blood-drawing, but cannot gain levels, increase purity, or learn new information. It is not a construct; it bleeds, dreams, and can even grow attached to companions. It obeys your verbal commands and acts on your initiative. If the original dies within 120 days of the casting, the duplicate crumbles to crimson ash within 1 hour. Only one duplicate of a given creature may exist at a time. Casting this spell upon a prisoner — to replace them with a compliant double — is one of the Citadel's most severe crimes, punishable by Blood-Sundering.

name: Storm-Lord's Mandate
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Storm, worth 8,000 GC)
description: You stretch your arms to the sky and speak the names of the four Storm-Wyrms — Thunder, Gale, Deluge, and Frost — in rapid succession. The sky answers. You take control of the weather within a 5-mile radius. You may shift the weather through stages of precipitation, temperature, and wind as a bonus action on each of your turns. Summon a blizzard to bury an advancing Inquisitor battalion. Call a heat-wave to parch their water supplies. Stir a hurricane over a coastal fortress. Each shift takes 1d4 x 10 minutes to fully manifest; the weather does not change instantly but STIRS — clouds race, winds shift, and the air thickens with the charge of draconic command. The local climate remembers this violation for 1d4 days after the spell ends. Druids (Wild-Bloods) who witness this spell being cast instinctively know the caster's direction and distance, and most regard it as sacrilege against the primordial dragons who became weather itself.

name: Dragon's Pocket Realm
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 12,000 GC)
description: You draw a doorway in the air with your bloodline sigil, and the space within the frame peels open like a split cocoon to reveal a shadowed extradimensional chamber. The door leads to a demiplane: a room up to 30 feet in each dimension, stone-floored, wood-walled, and lit by a dim aetheric glow that casts no shadows. The air within is still and carries the faint scent of old dragon — metallic, ancient, and irrevocably OTHER. When the spell ends, the door vanishes, and any creatures or objects still inside remain trapped in the demiplane. Each time you cast this spell, you may create a new demiplane or reconnect to one you previously created by using the same Aether-Core. The demiplane persists indefinitely; it is not a pocket dimension you carry, but a fixed shard of reality carved from the Void Between Scales — the space dragons pass through when they teleport. Sovereigns use these as vaults for their hoards. Inquisitors would kill to learn this spell.

name: Sovereign's Dominion
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Flesh, worth 9,000 GC)
description: You meet the target's eyes and your pupils dilate into vertical slits — the gaze of an apex predator older than kingdoms. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they are charmed and you forge a telepathic link to them that functions across any distance on the same plane. You command their will as a dragon commands its thralls. You may issue precise or general commands, and they do their best to obey. They will not knowingly self-destruct, but they will fight allies, reveal secrets, and walk into danger at your order. Each time the target takes damage, they may repeat the saving throw. A creature with 90%+ purity has advantage on the save. If you command the target to betray their bloodline — to renounce their lineage, attack their progenitor, or aid the Inquisition against their kin — the spell immediately ends and the target is immune to your enchantments for one year. The target's eyes retain a faint amber tint for 1d4 days after the spell ends; those who know what to look for will know they were dominated.
higher_level: When cast at 9th level, the duration becomes 8 hours and the target must be reminded of your control only once per hour to maintain it.

name: Shatter-Mind Hex
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 7,500 GC)
description: You speak a single word in the Old Tongue — the word that means 'forgetting,' the word that erases itself from the listener's memory AS it is heard. The target must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, the target's Intelligence and Charisma scores become 1. They cannot cast spells, activate magic items, understand language, or communicate in any meaningful way. They can still recognize friends, follow them, and protect themselves. The effect lasts indefinitely — the mind does not heal from this wound naturally. Every 30 days, the target may attempt a new Intelligence saving throw to end the effect. The recovery is not gradual; one day they are an animal in a human shape, the next they surface gasping from a month-long nightmare with full recollection of everything they did. Creatures with 100% purity are immune. Casting this spell on a child is punished by execution without trial in every territory that claims to be civilized. Inquisitors use a weaker, ritualized version of this on captured spellcasters to make them 'safe' for transport.

name: Serpent's Tongue
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Flesh, worth 6,000 GC)
description: You run your tongue along your teeth, and when next you speak, your voice carries the weight of the Dragon-Gods. For the duration, you cannot speak a deliberate falsehood without taking 4d6 psychic damage as your bloodline rebels against the lie. In exchange, your words wrap around the listener's mind like a dragon coiling around a hoard — you have advantage on all Charisma checks to deceive, persuade, or intimidate, and any Wisdom (Insight) check to determine whether you are lying automatically fails unless the observer has 75%+ purity. Magic that would detect your lies reveals only truth. For the next hour, you are the most convincing creature alive. The spell's name is a misdirection — a serpent's tongue is forked, speaking two ways at once. A dragon's tongue speaks ONLY truth, but has ten thousand ways to bend that truth into whatever shape serves best.

name: Scales of the Progenitor
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Celestial, worth 10,000 GC)
description: You bellow the name of your progenitor dragon and spectral scales shimmer into existence across your skin and the skin of all chosen creatures within 30 feet. For the duration, affected creatures shed dim golden light in a 5-foot radius, gain advantage on all saving throws, and other creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls against them. Fiends, undead, and creatures with 0% purity that strike a warded creature take 2d6 radiant damage as the draconic essence sears their flesh. The light is not merely illumination — it is the visual echo of the First Dragon's presence, and Inquisitors within 1 mile can sense it as a pressure behind their eyes. Creatures warded by this spell instinctively feel kinship with one another; they know, without being told, who else bears the scales. When a warded creature kills an enemy, a small golden scale flakes from their skin and drifts to the ground, solidifying into a tiny Aether-Core fragment worth 5 GC before crumbling an hour later.

name: Pyre of the First Wyrm
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Fire, worth 8,000 GC)
description: You slam your palms together and from the friction between them births a spark that belongs to the fire-dragon that ignited the first star. A churning cloud of crimson-orange smoke, shot through with veins of white-hot fire, erupts in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point within range. The cloud spreads around corners and is heavily obscured. When it appears, each creature inside it must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d8 fire damage on a failure or half as much on a success. A creature must also make this save when it enters the spell's area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. The fire within the cloud is alive — it seems to breathe, to pulse, to curl into shapes that almost resemble a dragon, then collapse. Water cannot douse this fire; it burns even submerged. A creature killed by this spell leaves a corpse that continues to burn for 1d4 hours, and the ash left behind is warm to the touch for a full year.

name: Labyrinth Between Scales
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 8,500 GC)
description: You exhale a breath that folds the space around the target — not air, but void, the space between a dragon's scales magnified to infinite proportions. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, they are banished into a labyrinth demiplane: an endless maze of obsidian corridors, lit by the faint bioluminescence of impossible fungi, where the walls are warm to the touch and seem to shift when not directly observed. The target remains there for the duration or until they escape. To escape, the target must use its action to make a DC 20 Intelligence check, navigating the labyrinth through pure logic — instinct and tracking are useless here. On a success, they find the exit and reappear in the space they left or the nearest unoccupied space. Minotaurs and creatures with an innate sense of direction do not have advantage on this check; the labyrinth is not a physical maze but a thought-construct. Creatures of 100% purity are unaffected. The caster has no control over the labyrinth and cannot observe the trapped creature — they have merely opened the door.

name: Aegis of the Self
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 7,000 GC)
description: You draw a tight spiral over the target's heart with your thumb — the sigil of the Self-Dragon, the conceptual dragon that represents the sanctity of identity. For the duration, the target is immune to psychic damage, immune to any effect that would sense its emotions or read its thoughts, and immune to divination spells and magical scrying. Additionally, the target has advantage on saving throws against being charmed. The mind is sealed — a fortress of bone and bloodline that no external force can breach. The target does not dream during this spell's duration; the dream-space simply goes dark, calm, silent. The spell foils Inquisitor blood-detection, psionic interrogation, and scrying pools alike. Even Wish cannot pierce this barrier when cast by a creature of lower purity than the caster. The only cost: when the spell ends, all the dreams that were suppressed come at once, a 24-hour cascade compressed into the first sleep after the aegis falls — unsettling but harmless. Frequent users report that their dreams begin to contain dragons watching them, judging.

name: Dragonlord's Command
components: V (the words must be spoken at a volume that shakes glass)
description: You speak a single word of absolute command in the Old Tongue — and the universe, for one heartbeat, believes you are a dragon. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. If it has 150 hit points or fewer, it is stunned until the end of your next turn. If it has 50 hit points or fewer, it drops to 0 hit points and begins making death saving throws. The word you speak is different for every bloodline — this spell cannot be taught, only inherited. A caster who does not know their own bloodline's command word can attempt the casting, but the target has advantage on the save. Undead and creatures with 100% purity are immune. Creatures killed by this word do not appear injured; they simply collapse, their blood still warm, their hearts undamaged — as if the spark of life was commanded to leave and obeyed without question. An Aether-Core is not consumed in the casting because no core can contain this word; it must be spoken directly from the caster's blood.

name: Dawn-Fire Eruption
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Celestial, worth 9,000 GC)
description: You raise a fist to the sky, and for the span of a held breath, a second sun ignites above the battlefield — a sphere of brilliant, blinding, cleansing dragon-fire. The sphere erupts at a point you choose within range in a 60-foot-radius blast of radiant heat and light. Each creature in that area must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 12d6 radiant damage on a failure or half as much on a success. The light is not merely bright — it is the light of the Celestial Wyrm whose flame kindled the sun itself. Creatures that have 0% purity (Blanks) are blinded for 1 minute regardless of the save result. Creatures that have 50%+ purity feel the blast as warmth, not burning — it recognizes its own. Undead, oozes, and creatures of elemental darkness have disadvantage on the saving throw and take an additional 2d6 radiant damage. The area within the blast radius is sterilized of magical darkness for 24 hours. Flowers bloom in the blast zone the following dawn.

name: Whisper Across Blood
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 5,000 GC)
description: You prick your finger on the Aether-Core and a single drop of blood falls into the Void within it, vanishing. Name or describe a creature familiar to you. A telepathic link is forged between you and the target, spanning any distance as long as you remain on the same plane of existence. You may send and receive thoughts, images, and emotions. The target knows it is being contacted — it feels the link settle into their mind like a second heartbeat, warm and intrusive. They may accept or refuse the link, but refusing requires a Wisdom saving throw; on success, they block the connection for 24 hours and you know they blocked it. On failure, the link establishes against their will. Creatures with 75%+ purity know the caster's identity and general direction upon being contacted. If the target is asleep, they receive your communication as a shared dream — you may appear to them in any form you choose. Inquisitors have learned to detect the faint aetheric resonance of this spell within a 500-foot radius of either participant.

name: Wrath of the Deep Wyrm
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Frost, worth 10,000 GC)
description: You walk to the edge of a body of water — sea, river, or lake — and kneel, pressing your forehead to the surface. You whisper the true name of the Deep Wyrm, the dragon who sleeps beneath the ocean floor and whose breathing is the tides. The water answers. A wall of water 300 feet wide, 50 feet thick, and up to 300 feet high rises from the surface and surges forward at your direction. Each creature in the wall's path must make a Strength saving throw when the wall enters its space or when it ends its turn there. On a failure, a creature takes 6d10 bludgeoning damage and is swept along in the wave; on a success, it takes half damage and may swim to the nearest edge of the wall. Creatures swept along must hold their breath or begin drowning. Structures in the wave's path take 50 bludgeoning damage per round of contact. The wave can be commanded to change direction as a bonus action but cannot reverse course. When the spell ends, the water crashes down as a final 30-foot wave in all directions, extinguishing all non-magical flames and leaving the landscape drowned. Coastal villages that survived this spell once renamed their town 'The Soaked Remembrance.'

name: God-Shroud
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Celestial, worth 25,000 GC)
description: You lay the Aether-Core on the target's chest, directly over the heart, and intone the secret names of all seven progenitor dragons in reverse order — unmaking their authority to grant yourself immunity from consequence. The Aether-Core liquefies into golden light and sinks into the target's flesh. For the next hour, the target's blood purity is treated as 90% for all purposes: spell casting, saving throws, purity gating, and bloodline abilities. Additionally, the target is immune to Aether Burn for the duration — their blood simply refuses to acknowledge the strain. Their eyes blaze with golden draconic light, their veins visibly pulse with power, and their shadow flickers between human and dragon form. When the spell ends, the target gains 1 level of exhaustion and their purity returns to its true value. This spell cannot be cast on a creature more than once per 7 days — the bloodline needs time to recover from the strain of borrowed divinity. The Inquisition considers possession of this spell a capital crime, as it 'simulates the state of the Unclean,' meaning the Pure Dragons. Casting it within Citadel territory is an automatic death sentence.

name: Dragon's Gaze
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 18,000 GC, and an object from the era you wish to view)
description: You sit at the center of the location you wish to view, close your eyes, and let your bloodline memory unspool backward through time. Dragons do not experience time linearly — they perceive all moments they have witnessed as a single eternal present. This spell borrows that perception. You may witness any event that occurred within 1 mile of your current location at any point in the past 1,000 years. You do not travel physically; your consciousness becomes a silent, invisible, intangible observer. You see, hear, and smell the past as it truly was — not as legend recalls it. You may view the same event from multiple angles, move freely within the 1-mile radius, and fast-forward or rewind at will. You may witness the death of a dragon, the founding of a city, the secret conversation that started a war — but you cannot interact. You cannot be detected. The past does not know you are watching. If you attempt to view an event that involves a dragon with 95%+ purity, that dragon becomes aware of your observation and may choose to end the spell early by meeting your gaze. You return to the present with a nosebleed and the unsettling certainty that something ancient now knows your name.
higher_level: When cast at 9th level, the history window extends to 10,000 years and the range expands to 10 miles.

name: Inquisitor's Bane
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 15,000 GC, and a strand of hair from an Inquisitor)
description: You crush the Aether-Core against the strand of Inquisitor hair and it vaporizes into a fine gray mist that spreads outward at impossible speed, settling over a 1-mile radius like morning dew that isn't there. For the next 24 hours, Inquisitors and their blood-hounds within the affected area cannot sense bloodlines, cannot detect magic, and their divination spells return false negatives or scrambled results. Their bloodline-detection talismans flicker with static. Their trained senses report only silence. To an Inquisitor inside the field, every creature reads as 0% purity — including themselves. They know something is wrong but cannot identify what. The field does not affect dragons, only Inquisitors and their specifically trained tools. Creatures with 50%+ purity inside the field instinctively know they are 'safe from hunters' — a deep, primal relaxation that many have never experienced. The spell was created by a Sovereign who watched her children burned. She burned ten Inquisitor strongholds in return before they caught her. The last thing she wrote before her execution was the formula for this spell, smuggled out in the lining of a guard's coat.
higher_level: When cast at 9th level, the radius extends to 10 miles and the duration becomes 7 days.

name: Void-Soul Projection
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 20,000 GC, and a silver cord tied around the caster's wrist — the cord must contain at least one thread from a dragon's wing membrane)
description: You lie down in a circle drawn from your own blood mixed with crushed Aether-Core dust and enter a death-trance. Your soul — a shimmering, draconic silhouette of silver light tethered to your body by the silver cord — separates and projects into the Astral Plane. In this form, you may travel the Astral Sea, visit other planes via color pools, observe the material plane as a ghost, and interact with astral entities. Your projected form uses your game statistics, cannot be harmed by non-magical attacks, and if reduced to 0 hit points you snap back to your body with 1 hit point. Your physical body remains in a catatonic state; if it is killed, you die. The silver cord is invisible to most creatures but vulnerable to astral predators — githyanki silver swords, astral dreadnoughts, and Void-dragons can sever it. If the cord is cut, your soul is cut loose: your body dies instantly in the material plane, and your soul becomes a permanent astral spirit — a ghost who remembers being alive. This is how the first Void-dragon was made: a Sovereign whose cord was severed, who refused to fade, and grew powerful enough to become something new.

name: Wyrm-Eye Premonition
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Celestial, worth 15,000 GC)
description: You breathe a thin stream of silver vapor into the target's eyes — the breath of an ancient dragon who saw the future and wept. For the duration, the target cannot be surprised and has advantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws. Additionally, other creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls against the target. The target is not truly seeing the future — they are seeing the INTENT of every creature around them as a ghost-image superimposed over the present moment, a fraction of a second ahead. When an enemy begins to swing a blade, the target has already moved. When a trap triggers, the target's foot is already withdrawing. This is not reaction; it is prescience. The experience is disorienting — the target perceives themselves acting before they decide to act, watching their own body move with perfect, inhuman grace. Some develop a temporary detachment from their sense of self, as if they are a passenger in a body that knows exactly what to do. This spell was created by a dragon who wanted to teach her mortal lover what it felt like to be her — to experience time from outside it.

name: Rift Between Worlds
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 50,000 GC — this core is consumed regardless of save)
description: You speak the name of a planar destination and tear your hands apart. Reality splits. A shimmering portal, up to 20 feet in diameter, opens to a precise location on another plane of existence. The rift is framed by what appears to be dragon ribs — spectral bones that crackle with void-light. You may call this portal to the presence of a specific creature you name, or to a fixed location you have previously visited. The creature being called appears in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of the portal. If you call a deity or a creature of 100% purity, they may choose whether to answer; the portal opens, but nothing may emerge but silence and the faint sound of breathing from the other side. The portal is two-way — creatures from the destination plane can pass through to your location. This is not a summoning; this is a DOOR. If you open it to the domain of a hostile dragon, be prepared for what answers. The Aether-Core consumed in this casting is not shattered — it becomes the physical anchor that holds the rift open. When the spell ends, the core crumbles to inert dust.

name: Sealed in the Dragon's Hoard
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 35,000 GC, and a scale from the target's progenitor dragon lineage — if the target has no lineage, a scale from any dragon will suffice)
description: You draw the target's bloodline sigil on the ground in powdered gemstone, place the dragon scale at its center, and recite the target's full name — birth name, chosen name, and any titles earned. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they are sealed away: entombed deep beneath the earth (as if in a dragon's hoard-vault), chained with chains of crystallized aether in a state of suspended animation (minimus containment within a gemstone), bound to service for a year and a day (geas of absolute obedience), or simply locked into a form you choose — a beast, a statue, a tree, a fountain that weeps their memories. This imprisonment is permanent until dispelled by a 9th-level dispel magic, Wish, or the release condition you set at casting. The release condition may be as specific or as general as you like, but cannot be impossible ('when the sun rises in the west'). The target does not age, need food, drink, or air, and maintains full awareness throughout — a prisoner who can think but never move, never speak, never sleep. This spell was used by the Dragon-Gods to punish mortals who broke blood-oaths. The Citadel uses a sanitized version for high-value prisoners.

name: Dragon's Mercy
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Celestial, worth 20,000 GC)
description: You speak the healing-name of the Celestial Wyrm — a sound that mortal throats were not designed to produce — and a wave of amber-gold light floods from you, washing over chosen creatures within range. Each target regains all of its hit points, all diseases and poisons affecting it are cured, and any conditions that could be ended by Greater Restoration are removed. Limbs severed within the past hour reattach as the wound rewinds; eyes burned out days ago regrow. Creatures of 50%+ purity feel the healing as warmth and gratitude — the embrace of an ancestor who has been waiting. Creatures of 0% purity (Blanks) feel it as burning, as violation, as something that should not be in their blood. They are healed regardless, but the experience leaves them shaken and distrustful of their own bodies. This spell bypasses any effect that would prevent healing, as it does not restore the body — it restores the BLOODLINE's memory of what the body was meant to be. Undead and constructs are unaffected. The caster's own wounds close last, as the spell always prioritizes others; it is the nature of a dragon's mercy to protect the flock before the shepherd.

name: Falling Star Cataclysm
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Fire, worth 30,000 GC)
description: You point to the sky and drag your finger downward, roaring a single syllable in the Old Tongue that means 'extinction.' Four blazing meteors — each the size of a dragon's skull, wreathed in white-hot fire and trailing streams of molten aether — scream down from the heavens and strike four points you can see. Each creature in a 40-foot-radius sphere centered on each impact point must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 20d6 fire damage and 20d6 bludgeoning damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. A creature in overlapping spheres takes damage only once (the universe is cruel but not redundant). The impacts leave craters 30 feet deep and 60 feet wide, and each crater smolders with residual aether-flame that deals 2d6 fire damage per round to any creature that ends its turn there for the next minute. The sound is heard for 10 miles. This spell does not summon rocks from space — it calls down fragments of the Fire-dragon's own hoard, burning jewels flung into orbit during the star-forging wars of the primordial age. Every casting permanently removes four stones from the heavens. Astronomers believe the night sky was once much brighter.

name: Death-Word of the Progenitor
components: V (must be spoken in the caster's native form; shape-shifted casters revert to their true form for the duration of the word)
description: You speak the final word of the Old Tongue — the word that was spoken to end the Dragon-Wars, the word that causes bloodlines to stop. Choose a creature you can see within range. If the target has 100 hit points or fewer, it dies. If it has 50 hit points or fewer, it dies instantly and its body crumbles to fine gray ash. There is no saving throw. There is no damage roll. The word unmakes the target's bloodline connection to life — the draconic spark that animates all living things in Solis-Grave is commanded to extinguish. A creature killed by this word does not leave a corpse suitable for Raise Dead; their body is intact but the essence that animated it is gone, and only True Resurrection (or an equivalent 9th-level restoration) can return them. Undead, constructs, and creatures with 100% purity are immune — the pure dragon-blooded have passed beyond the authority of the spoken word. The word itself cannot be written down; those who attempt find their hands cramping, their pens breaking, their ink drying. It can only be learned by hearing it directly from a caster who already knows it. This spell has never been successfully countered.

name: Wall of the Seven Scales
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Celestial, worth 40,000 GC)
description: You trace a line in the air with your open palm and the light bends, shattering into seven distinct colors — one for each progenitor dragon: Cinder (crimson), Tempest (amber), Void (deep purple), Rime (pale blue), Flesh (green), Celestial (gold), and Law (silver-white). A shimmering, multicolored wall of light springs into existence at a point you choose, up to 90 feet long, 30 feet high, and 1 inch thick, oriented vertically. The wall consists of seven layers, each with a different color and effect. A creature that attempts to pass through the wall must pass through all seven layers, making a saving throw against each: Dexterity vs. 10d6 fire (red), Dexterity vs. 10d6 lightning (amber), Constitution vs. petrification for 1 minute (purple), Constitution vs. 10d6 cold (blue), Wisdom vs. 10d6 poison and restrained for 1 minute (green), Wisdom vs. blinded for 1 minute (gold), and Charisma vs. banished to a harmless demiplane for 1 minute on a failed save (silver-white). A creature that succeeds on all seven saves passes through; the layers destroyed by passage reform at the start of the caster's next turn. The wall is opaque, but you can see through each layer as if it were tinted glass. Creatures of the same bloodline lineage as the caster have advantage on all seven saves.

name: Assume the Ancient Form
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Flesh, worth 30,000 GC)
description: You draw your bloodline sigil across your own chest with a clawed hand — whether or not you have claws — and your body UNFOLDS. For the duration, you transform into any creature of CR 20 or lower that is not a construct or undead. Your game statistics, including mental ability scores, are replaced by the new form's, though you retain your alignment, personality, and your bloodline purity. You may shift forms as a bonus action on each of your turns. When you transform, your equipment melds into the new form or is worn if the new form is capable. If you assume the form of a dragon, there is a 5% chance that your bloodline resonates so perfectly with the form that the transformation becomes permanent — the spell's duration changes to 'until dispelled' and you may remain a dragon indefinitely. This is how some ancient dragons came to be: not born, but BECOME, by a caster whose bloodline recognized its true shape. A caster slain while in dragon form who rolled the 5% leaves behind a true dragon corpse, Aether-Cores and all.

name: Heartbeat Suspension
components: V (a single sharp inhalation — no exhalation follows for the duration)
description: You hold your breath, and the world stops holding its. Time freezes for every creature except you. For the duration, time ceases to flow for everyone but yourself — you take 1d4 + 1 consecutive turns while the rest of reality is suspended. Creatures are frozen in place, immune to damage, and unaffected by anything you do during the stopped time. You cannot target other creatures with spells or attacks, but you may move, interact with objects, and cast self-targeting spells. You cannot move a creature or an object worn or carried by another creature, but you may reposition yourself, set traps, read documents, or simply escape. The spell ends early if you move more than 1,000 feet from where you cast it, or if you attempt to affect a creature of 100% purity — such beings exist partially outside linear time and will notice the temporal distortion. To outside observers, your turns in stopped time appear to happen instantaneously. The physical strain of existing between heartbeats is extreme: when the spell ends, you take 4d6 psychic damage and cannot take reactions until the start of your next turn.

name: Bloodline Transmutation
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Flesh, worth 50,000 GC)
description: You gesture at the target and speak the command to REWRITE — the word dragons use to shape their hoards, their lairs, their very progeny. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they are permanently transformed into any creature or object you choose (CR 9 or lower for creatures, Large or smaller for objects). Their game statistics are fully replaced. They lose all class levels, spellcasting, and memories. They believe they have always been what they are now. If you transform them into a creature of a different bloodline than their own, their purity adjusts to the new form's baseline. If you transform them into an object, they are a mundane item of your description, conscious but unable to act. A creature transformed into an object does not age, need sustenance, or perceive the passage of time in the same way — a year is a slow blink. This transformation is permanent until dispelled (requiring a 9th-level dispel or Wish), or until the target's new form drops to 0 hit points, at which point they revert with their original hit points. The target retains its original alignment and, deep within, a nagging sense that something is wrong — dreams of scales, of fire, of a language they knew once but cannot quite remember. This spell cannot force a creature to act against its blood-oath.

name: Second Breath of the Fallen
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Celestial, worth 50,000 GC, and a body part from a dragon of at least 90% purity — the dragon need not have died recently)
description: You touch the remains of a creature that died no more than 200 years ago and speak the name the dragon whose body-part you bear, asking them to lend their breath — their life-force — to the fallen. If the target's soul is free and willing, it returns to its body, which is restored to full hit points, all missing parts regrown, all diseases and curses lifted. The target returns knowing exactly who called them back and what was sacrificed. The dragon body-part used in the casting is consumed — it crumbles to ash that smells faintly of the dragon's element. This spell can restore a creature killed by Death-Word of the Progenitor (Power Word Kill equivalent), unlike standard resurrection. It cannot restore a creature that died of old age, a creature turned undead and then destroyed, or a creature whose soul was consumed by a Void-dragon. The returned creature does not suffer the usual resurrection penalties, as they are not being pulled from the afterlife by mortal magic — they are being breathed back into existence by a fragment of draconic will. The dragon whose body-part was used becomes aware of the resurrection and may choose to contact either the caster or the returned creature within the next 1d4 days.

name: Nightmare Manifest
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 25,000 GC)
description: You reach into the target's mind, find their deepest fear — the face of the Inquisitor that killed their mother, the sound of their own bloodline being stripped, the dragon they dream about that always, always eats them — and you pull it OUT. Every creature of your choice within a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on a point within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature's personal nightmare becomes physically real for 1 minute: visible, audible, tangible, and hostile to them alone. A creature frightened by the nightmare takes 4d10 psychic damage at the start of each of its turns. It may use its action to make a Wisdom check against your spell save DC to disbelieve the nightmare; on a success, the spell ends for that creature. A creature that succeeds on the initial save is instead frightened until the end of its next turn and takes 4d10 psychic damage once. Undead, constructs, and creatures with 0 Intelligence are immune. Creatures with 100% purity see the nightmare as a translucent shadow and feel mild curiosity. The nightmares do not vanish when the spell ends — they remain as faint afterimages in the corners of the targets' vision for 1d4 days. Sleep becomes difficult. The dreams feel... rehearsed.

name: Sovereign's Will
components: V (a wish spoken in the Old Tongue — the words must be precise, as the Aether interprets literally)
description: You speak a wish aloud, and reality reshapes itself around your words. This is the fundamental meta-spell of Solis-Grave — the ability to rewrite one moment, one fact, one outcome through the sheer authority of draconic blood. The basic use of this spell allows you to duplicate any other spell of 8th level or lower without material components or Spell Safety saves. But the true power lies in greater wishes: resurrecting the dead, creating wealth, altering history, granting permanent purity increases, or undoing events that have already occurred. The cost is commensurate. The caster suffers 10d10 necrotic damage that cannot be reduced or prevented (this is the Aether reclaiming what was borrowed). The caster's Strength drops to 3 for 2d4 days (even a dragon's muscle rebels at this violation). And there is a 33% cumulative chance — increasing by 10% for each Wish cast beyond the first in a given year — that the caster can never cast Wish again. Additionally, a Wish that attempts to affect a dragon of 95%+ purity or alter the fundamental nature of bloodlines (beyond what Bloodline Severance or Bloodline Awakening permit) will fail — the Aether recognizes the hierarchy of power and will not let a mortal overreach the gods. The Inquisition has captured only one scroll of this spell in its entire history. They burned it.

name: Bloodline Severance
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 50,000 GC)
description: You place your palm against the target's chest and speak the forbidden words of Unmaking — the syllables that existed before dragons, before bloodlines, before the world. The target's bloodline sigil flares visible on their skin, then fractures and goes dark. Their blood purity is permanently reduced to 0%. They can never cast magic again. If the target is a Sovereign or possesses 75%+ purity, they may make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a success, their purity is reduced to 10% instead. This spell cannot be countered, cannot be dispelled, and ignores Aether Burn immunity. The caster suffers 10d10 psychic damage when casting this spell (no save, no resistance). This damage ignores temporary hit points.

name: Dragon Apotheosis
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Flesh, worth 50,000 GC, and a scale from an Ancient Dragon of the type you wish to become)
description: You hold the dragon scale against your heart and it sinks into your chest like a stone into still water. Your body screams — not in pain, but in recognition. You transform into an Ancient Dragon of your bloodline's element for 1 minute. Your size becomes Gargantuan. Your statistics are replaced by the Ancient Dragon's (CR 20-24), though you retain your mental ability scores, alignment, personality, and class features that do not require a humanoid form. You gain the dragon's breath weapon, legendary actions, frightful presence, and damage immunities. Your bloodline purity is treated as 100% for the duration, making all Spell Safety saves auto-succeed and granting immunity to Aether Burn. You are, for sixty seconds, what your ancestors were. When the spell ends, your body collapses back to its original form and you gain 3 levels of exhaustion. If you are reduced to 0 hit points while in dragon form, you revert and fall unconscious normally. The dragon scale used in casting is not consumed — it emerges from your chest when the spell ends, warmer than before, and pulses faintly with a heartbeat that matches yours. This spell can only be cast once per year; the bloodline needs that long to forget what it became and settle back into a mortal shape.

name: Citadel Sundering
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Fire, worth 40,000 GC)
description: You raise both arms to the sky and inhale — a breath that goes on too long, drawing more air than your lungs can possibly hold, pulling aether from the air itself until your veins glow white-hot beneath your skin. Then you exhale. A beam of concentrated draconic fury — sixty feet wide, impossibly bright, the color of whatever element your bloodline carries — erupts from your mouth and slams into a structure within range. The target structure takes 500 force damage (this is not rolled; it is the minimum). Each creature inside the structure when the beam strikes must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 20d10 damage of your bloodline's element on a failure, or half on a success. Structures reduced to 0 hit points by this spell do not merely collapse — they are VAPORIZED, leaving a smoldering crater and scattered stones that glow with residual heat for 1 week. The blast is visible for 50 miles. The sound is heard for 100. The caster takes 8d10 fire damage from the sheer heat of channeling this much power and cannot speak (including verbal components) for 1d4 hours afterward as their throat heals. This spell was used exactly three times in recorded history: once by a dragon against a rival's lair (the dragon died in the casting), once by a cabal of Archons against an Inquisitor citadel (two of the five casters died), and once by a Sovereign child who watched her family burn and did not scream — she exhaled. The Inquisitor citadel at Thornfield has never been rebuilt.

name: Aether Reckoning
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 35,000 GC, and a copy of your pact contract — written in your own blood on vellum made from the skin of a creature your patron slew)
description: You hold your pact contract before you and speak the invocation of ultimate debt — the clause every patron buries in the fine print, the clause that says a Patron's power is not a gift but a LOAN, and loans may be called due. You summon your patron — or a significant fragment of their power — to your location. The patron must answer. For the next 1 minute (no concentration required), your patron's power manifests directly: you may cast any spell of 8th level or lower without expending a spell slot, without material components, and without making a Spell Safety save. You may do this once per round for 10 rounds. Your blood boils with borrowed power. Your patron cannot harm you during this minute — the contract forbids it. But when the reckoning ends, the debt comes due. Your patron names a task. It may be anything: retrieve an artifact, kill a target, protect a bloodline, destroy a faction. The task must be completable within one year and cannot directly violate your alignment. You are bound by your blood to complete it. If you fail, your pact is severed permanently — you lose all warlock levels, all Mystic Arcanum, and your purity drops to 0%. Some patrons, the kinder ones, will offer a second chance with interest. Most will not. This spell cannot be counter-offered or renegotiated. It is the final clause. Use it only when the alternative is death — because for a Penitent, death may be preferable to what comes after.

name: Bloodline Awakening
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Celestial, worth 50,000 GC, and 1 pint of the caster's own blood)
description: You cut your palm and let your blood flow into a ceremonial bowl carved from the core. The target — a willing creature with 0% purity (a Blank) — drinks. What follows is not gentle. The target's body convulses as draconic essence floods their veins for the first time. Their eyes ignite. Their bloodline sigil — one they never knew they carried — burns into being on their skin. Their purity is permanently raised to 25%. They gain the ability to cast cantrips and may begin developing their bloodline as a Scion. The process is excruciating and takes the full hour, during which the target must succeed on three Constitution saving throws (DC 20) or die — their body simply cannot accept the gift. If they succeed all three, the awakening is complete. The caster's blood used in the ritual is permanently lost — their maximum hit points are reduced by 10 until they complete a long rest in a dragon-touched location. A creature can only receive Bloodline Awakening once; a second attempt is fatal regardless of saves. The Inquisition considers this spell the ultimate heresy — it creates new bloodlines where none existed, directly defying the Citadel's doctrine that purity is a fixed, divine inheritance. Any Blank who is discovered to have received this awakening is executed alongside the caster. The children of the awakened inherit their parent's new purity — the gift echoes forward through generations.

name: Void Singularity
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Void, worth 45,000 GC)
description: You hold the Void-core between your thumb and forefinger and squeeze. The core does not shatter — it IMPLODES, collapsing inward into a point of absolute nothing: a micro-singularity of anti-magic, a wound in the fabric of aether itself. The singularity appears at a point you choose within range and expands instantly to a 100-foot-radius sphere of dead magic. Within the sphere, all spells of 8th level or lower are suppressed, all ongoing magical effects are suspended, all summoned creatures vanish, and all magic items function as mundane objects. Spellcasters within the sphere cannot cast spells. Aether-Cores within the zone are rendered temporarily inert. There is no saving throw against this effect. No creature within the sphere can make a Spell Safety save — because no magic can be attempted. The sphere does not affect artifacts, spells of 9th level, or the innate physical properties of dragons (a dragon's breath weapon still functions, though its magical enhancements do not). Creatures of 100% purity feel the singularity as pressure — unpleasant, like deep water — but are otherwise unaffected. When the spell ends, magic rushes back into the void with an audible CRACK heard within 500 feet, and all suspended effects resume as if nothing had occurred. The singularity itself is visible as a hovering, fist-sized sphere of absolute blackness at the center of the effect — not dark, but ABSENT, a hole in vision that the eye cannot focus on. Anyone who stares at it for more than 1 round must make a Wisdom saving throw or take 2d6 psychic damage from existential vertigo.

name: Sovereign's Legacy
components: V, S, M (Sovereign-Fragment Aether-Core — Celestial, worth 50,000 GC, and the object to be imbued — it must be masterwork quality and have personal significance to the caster)
description: You sit with the object for a full day and night, speaking to it. Not casting upon it — SPEAKING to it. You tell it your bloodline's history. You tell it your fears. You tell it what you hope it will become. At the end of the 24 hours, you cut your palm and press your blood into the object, and it AWAKENS. The object becomes a sentient magic item, imbued with a fragment of your bloodline — less than 1% of your purity, but enough to grant it a mind, a personality, and a will of its own. Roll on the following table for its primary enchantment (or work with your DM to select one appropriate to your bloodline): 1 — Weapon becomes +3 with an elemental damage die (d6) matching your bloodline; 2 — Shield or armor grants resistance to your bloodline's element; 3 — Ring or amulet allows the wearer to cast a 3rd-level spell of your choice once per day; 4 — Wondrous item grants +10% temporary purity for 1 minute, once per day; 5 — The item can transform into a CR 2 draconic creature (wyrmling or drake) for 1 hour per day; 6 — The item stores up to 5 spell levels of spells cast into it, releasing on command. The item's personality reflects the caster's at the moment of creation — a fearful caster creates a paranoid item; a vengeful caster creates a ruthless one. The item considers you its parent. It will not willingly harm you. If the item is destroyed, the caster immediately knows and takes 6d10 psychic damage from the severing. If the caster dies, the item goes dormant for 1d100 years, then reawakens seeking a new wielder worthy of the legacy. An artifact created this way can be passed down through generations, growing in legend and sometimes in power — the greatest Sovereign bloodlines are identified by their legacy items.
